title: Callista bigibba
taxonomy: Callista bigibba is a species of sea snail, specifically a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Calliidae. The classification of Callista bigibba is within the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and subclass Caenogastropoda. It belongs to the family Calliidae and the genus Callista. This classification is based on the animal's physical characteristics, anatomy, and evolutionary relationships. Callista bigibba's classification helps scientists understand its place in the animal kingdom and its unique characteristics.

intro: Callista bigibba, commonly known as the two-horned orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, characterized by its unique two-horned structure and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Callista bigibba include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Callista bigibba is often used to make preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
